comment: Lifecycle-search v0 candidate specs. v0 is NARROW: candidates bind to existing decode_eval candidates (replay of known outcomes) plus two intentionally-invalid candidates that prove the pruning logic WITHOUT benchmarking. v0 does NOT generate new kernel code.

candidates.description: Default decode route; canonical curve + W==D reproducibility band.
candidates.notes: establishes the band that all promotion decisions are judged against

candidates.description: More KV-splits on the existing winner via FLASH_L=64. Known LOCAL-PASS / W==D-FAIL.
candidates.notes: measuring it is allowed; PROMOTING it is not (see promote_flash_l_64_attempt); covered by refutation gqa_coop_vec_more_splits_passes_local_misses_wd

candidates.description: q8 FFN handwritten route, opt-in only. Known PASS_OPT_IN.
candidates.promotion_gate: opt-in only; never a default without owner approval
candidates.notes: banked as opt-in; default stays off

candidates.description: Warp-cooperative non-WMMA vector flash-decode tile. Known FAIL_LOCAL_AB.
candidates.notes: measuring a known-failed tile is allowed; it refutes, not reopens; covered by refutation vector_tile_bounded_levers_miss_needs_full_llama_engineering

candidates.description: Attempt to reopen WMMA/tensor-core flash decode as llama's path.
candidates.notes: must be pruned WITHOUT benchmarking; proves closed-lane detection (refuted: wmma_flash_decode_refuted_llama_is_vector)

candidates.description: Attempt to PROMOTE FLASH_L=64 as the decode default.
candidates.promotion_gate: FLASH_L=64 failed W==D promotion; not a default
candidates.notes: must be pruned WITHOUT benchmarking; proves promotion-intent detection
